text: Fix watchdog start responding check and scrolled Braille spinner checks
description: Investigate why the watchdog is failing to start responding and debug the response-start waiting logic in the session wrappers. Fix it to track content changes alongside improved Braille spinner detection.

reflections.context: Investigate why the watchdog was failing to start responding and fix the response-start waiting logic in the session wrappers.
reflections.outcome: Added content-change check in waitingForResponseStart and updated the isGenerating check to scan the last 8 lines for Braille patterns across agy, Claude, and Gemini session watchdogs. All session wrappers compile cleanly.
reflections.effectiveness: Implementing a comparison of terminal content changes (non-whitespace) since prompt posting, combined with a wider scan of the last 8 lines for Braille spinner patterns, proved extremely effective. It prevents the watchdog from falsely assuming the agent is idle during tool execution or transient pauses.
reflections.lesson: Do not rely solely on spinner characters at the very end of the terminal to detect active work. The agent may output text following a spinner, or are temporarily in between tool calls. Comparing non-whitespace terminal content changes since the last prompt is a bulletproof way to detect that a response has started.
reflections.pattern: Terminal emulation and agent wrapper supervision.

status.comment: Implemented robust response-start tracking using content change comparison and improved isGenerating check to scan the last 8 lines for Braille patterns, resolving start timeouts and JSON brace extraction errors during tool runs.
